claim_text: LoCoMo is not a purely naturalistic corpus because it is generated through an LLM pipeline and then materially revised by human annotators.
mechanism_text: The corpus is produced by a constructed generation pipeline in which agent personas are expanded by an LLM and the resulting conversations are later corrected by human annotators, so benchmark coherence is partly created through scaffolded generation plus post hoc repair rather than raw observation of natural dialogue.
bounds: This supports a mechanism for how the benchmark achieves long-range coherence, but it also limits claims about ecological realism because nearly 15% of turns and about 19% of images required human intervention.

claim_text: Retrieval helps on LoCoMo when it is selective and observation-focused, but more retrieved context or longer history can degrade performance.
mechanism_text: Selective retrieval of turn-level observations appears to preserve more task-relevant memory cues than broader dialog or summary retrieval, while larger retrieval sets or longer dialogue history appear to introduce noise or relevance dilution that reduces downstream performance.
bounds: The evidence supports this as an operational pattern on the benchmark, not as a universal law of memory systems, and it does not isolate the full causal share of retrieval noise versus other factors.
